docs.title: Schedula FastAPI Documentation
docs.text:
Schedula FastAPI is a stateless scheduling backend that builds course timetables using OR-Tools CP-SAT and data from MongoDB.

This documentation is the source of truth for:

  • service architecture and request flow
  • API endpoints and payloads
  • model and collection contracts
  • runtime configuration and environment variables
  • tests, CI, and deployment workflows

docs.title: What This Service Does
docs.text:
• reads institution, courses, staff, availability, rooms, and weight configuration
  • builds a constraint model for hard and soft scheduling rules
  • solves and returns a schedule snapshot to the client
  • does not persist generated snapshots (persistence is handled externally)

docs.title: Documentation Map
docs.text:
• Quickstart: local setup and development loop
  • Architecture: internals, modules, and solver flow
  • API Reference: route contracts with examples
  • Data Models: Pydantic and MongoDB data shape reference
  • Configuration: all settings from .env
  • Testing: unit/integration test strategy and commands
  • Deployment: production deployment and Pages/Azure notes
  • CI/CD Workflows: GitHub Actions inventory and behavior

docs.title: Key Runtime Endpoints
docs.text:
• GET / service entry metadata
  • GET /health liveness
  • GET /health/ready readiness with MongoDB ping
  • POST /schedule/generate schedule generation

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: Base URL (local): http://localhost:8000

docs.title: GET /
docs.text: Returns service metadata.

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
{
    "message": "Schedula Schedule Solver API",
    "docs": "/docs",
    "health": "/health"
}

docs.title: GET /health
docs.text: Liveness endpoint.

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
{
    "status": "healthy",
    "timestamp": "2026-03-24T00:00:00.000000+00:00"
}

docs.title: GET /health/ready
docs.text: Readiness endpoint with MongoDB connectivity check.

docs.title: Ready Response
docs.text:
{
    "status": "ready",
    "database": "connected"
}

docs.title: Not Ready Response
docs.text:
Status: 503

{
    "status": "not_ready",
    "database": "disconnected",
    "error": "..."
}

docs.title: POST /schedule/generate
docs.text: Generate a schedule snapshot from MongoDB input data.

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text:
{
    "institution_id": "test-inst-001",
    "term_label": "fall-2024",
    "weights": {
        "break_window": 100,
        "consecutive_slots": 80,
        "session_spread": 60,
        "campus_clustering": 40
    },
    "section_type_durations": {
        "lecture": 120,
        "lab": 180,
        "tutorial": 60
    }
}

Notes:

  • weights is optional
  • section_type_durations is optional
  • weight precedence is request > constraints collection > app defaults

docs.title: Success Response (200)
docs.text:
{
    "snapshot_id": "f0f2b9fe-8cb0-4f0b-b149-2a204ac4aa8f",
    "institution_id": "test-inst-001",
    "term_label": "fall-2024",
    "generated_at": "2026-03-24T00:00:00.000000+00:00",
    "entries": [
        {
            "section_id": "sec-cs101-lec",
            "course_name": "Intro to CS",
            "section_type": "lecture",
            "day_of_week": 1,
            "start_time": "09:00",
            "end_time": "10:00",
            "room_id": "room-101",
            "assigned_staff": ["prof-001"]
        }
    ],
    "hard_violations": 0,
    "soft_penalty": 320.0,
    "warnings": [],
    "summary": {
        "total_sections": 3,
        "scheduled_sections": 3,
        "total_staff": 2,
        "total_rooms": 2,
        "weights": {
            "break_window": 100,
            "consecutive_slots": 80,
            "session_spread": 60,
            "campus_clustering": 40
        }
    }
}

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text:
• 404: institution not found
  • 400: no courses found for institution
  • 500: solver/runtime failure

docs.title: Constraint Behavior
docs.text:
Hard constraints include:

  • H1: room no-overlap
  • H2: staff no-overlap
  • H3: room capacity filtering
  • H4: room label compatibility filtering
  • H5: weekly staff day off
  • H8: year-level conflict prevention

Soft objective terms include:

  • S1: preferred break-window overlap penalty
  • S2: back-to-back session penalty
  • S3: same-day multi-session penalty for a section
  • S4: per-day campus attendance penalty per staff

docs.title: OpenAPI UI
docs.text:
Use FastAPI interactive docs for live schema inspection:

  • Swagger UI: /docs
  • OpenAPI JSON: /openapi.json

docs.title: High-Level Flow
docs.text:
1. Client calls POST /schedule/generate
  2. API reads required data from MongoDB collections
  3. ScheduleSolver builds a CP-SAT model
  4. Solver computes a feasible/optimal schedule
  5. API returns snapshot payload (stateless response)

docs.title: Module Layout
docs.text:
• app/main.py: FastAPI app, lifespan hooks, CORS, router mounting
  • app/config.py: environment-backed settings
  • app/database/client.py: async MongoDB init/close and dependency provider
  • app/database/queries.py: typed query helpers for each collection
  • app/routes/health.py: liveness/readiness routes
  • app/routes/solver.py: schedule generation endpoint and orchestration
  • app/services/solver_service.py: OR-Tools model + constraints + solve
  • app/models/*.py: request/response and domain data models

docs.title: Lifespan and DB Lifecycle
docs.text:
Startup:

  • init_db() creates AsyncMongoClient
  • ping check validates connectivity

Shutdown:

  • close_db() closes client

get_db() is injected into routes using FastAPI dependency injection.

docs.title: Solver Pipeline
docs.text:
Inside ScheduleSolver.build_model(...):

  1. Build time-grid metadata from institution settings
  2. Pre-compute compatible rooms per section (capacity + label)
  3. Pre-compute staff-to-sections index
  4. Create session variables and interval variables
  5. Add hard constraints
  6. Add soft penalties and objective minimization

Inside solve():

  1. Configure CP-SAT runtime (max_time_in_seconds, workers)
  2. Solve model
  3. Convert variable values into schedule entries
  4. Return (hard_violations, soft_penalty, entries)

docs.title: Constraint Model Summary
docs.text:
Hard constraints:

  • H1 room no-overlap via AddNoOverlap on room optional intervals
  • H2 staff no-overlap via AddNoOverlap on staff intervals
  • H3 room capacity room filtering
  • H4 room label compatibility filtering
  • H5 weekly day-off blocking
  • H8 year-level conflict separation

Soft constraints:

  • S1 break-window overlap penalty
  • S2 adjacent-session penalty
  • S3 same-day repeated-section penalty
  • S4 staff campus-day penalty

docs.title: Time Representation
docs.text:
For each session occurrence:

  • day: 0..N-1 over institution working days
  • slot: start slot within day
  • abs_s: absolute start in week timeline
  • abs_e: absolute end in week timeline

This flattened timeline enables efficient overlap constraints.

docs.title: Stateless Contract
docs.text: The backend returns generated snapshots but does not persist them. Persistence and approval workflows are intentionally external to this API service.

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text: Keep a short list of notable changes here.

docs.title: Unreleased
docs.text:
• Replaced placeholder docs with comprehensive project documentation.
  • Added dedicated pages for architecture, models, configuration, testing, and workflows.
  • Switched docs CI/CD to MkDocs-specific GitHub Pages workflow (mkdocs-pages.yml).
  • Removed obsolete docs workflow file and aligned Pages publishing guidance.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Runtime settings are defined in app/config.py using pydantic-settings and loaded from .env.

docs.title: FastAPI
docs.text:
• APP_NAME (default: Schedula Schedule Solver)
  • DEBUG (default: false)
  • CORS_ORIGINS (default: [*])
  • LOG_LEVEL (default: INFO)

docs.title: MongoDB
docs.text:
• MONGODB_URI (required in production)
  • MONGODB_DB_NAME (default: schedula)

docs.title: Solver Runtime
docs.text:
• SOLVER_TIME_LIMIT_SECONDS (default: 55)
  • SOLVER_NUM_WORKERS (default: 8)

docs.title: Default Soft Weights
docs.text:
Used when neither request-level nor DB-level weight overrides are supplied.

  • SOFT_WEIGHT_BREAK_WINDOW (default: 100)
  • SOFT_WEIGHT_CONSECUTIVE_SLOTS (default: 80)
  • SOFT_WEIGHT_SESSION_SPREAD (default: 60)
  • SOFT_WEIGHT_CAMPUS_CLUSTERING (default: 40)

docs.title: Example .env
docs.text:
APP_NAME=Schedula Schedule Solver
DEBUG=true
CORS_ORIGINS=["*"]
LOG_LEVEL=INFO

MONGODB_URI=mongodb+srv://<user>:<password>@<cluster>/<db>?retryWrites=true&w=majority
MONGODB_DB_NAME=schedula

SOLVER_TIME_LIMIT_SECONDS=55
SOLVER_NUM_WORKERS=8

SOFT_WEIGHT_BREAK_WINDOW=100
SOFT_WEIGHT_CONSECUTIVE_SLOTS=80
SOFT_WEIGHT_SESSION_SPREAD=60
SOFT_WEIGHT_CAMPUS_CLUSTERING=40

docs.title: Weight Resolution Order
docs.text:
For /schedule/generate, final weights are resolved in this order:

  1. request body weights
  2. MongoDB constraints collection
  3. environment defaults (SOFT_WEIGHT_*)

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• Keep MONGODB_URI in secrets, never in source control.
  • SOLVER_NUM_WORKERS should match runtime CPU availability for best throughput.
  • Set restrictive CORS_ORIGINS in production.

docs.title: Data Models
docs.text: This page summarizes model contracts used by the API and solver.

docs.title: GenerateScheduleRequest
docs.text:
• institution_id: str
  • term_label: str
  • weights: SolverWeights | null
  • section_type_durations: SectionTypeDurations | null

docs.title: SolverWeights
docs.text:
• break_window: int >= 0 (default 100)
  • consecutive_slots: int >= 0 (default 80)
  • session_spread: int >= 0 (default 60)
  • campus_clustering: int >= 0 (default 40)

docs.title: SectionTypeDurations
docs.text:
Optional per-type duration overrides in minutes:

  • lecture
  • lab
  • tutorial

docs.title: GenerateScheduleResponse
docs.text:
• snapshot_id: str
  • institution_id: str
  • term_label: str
  • generated_at: str
  • entries: ScheduleEntryResponse[]
  • hard_violations: int
  • soft_penalty: float
  • warnings: str[]
  • summary: SummaryResponse

docs.title: Institution
docs.text:
• _id, name, slug
  • working_days: int[]
  • daily_start_hour, daily_end_hour
  • slot_duration_minutes

docs.title: CourseSection
docs.text:
• _id, institution_id, department_id
  • course_name, section_type
  • year_levels[]
  • slots_per_week, slot_duration_minutes
  • capacity, required_room_label
  • assigned_staff[], shared_with[]

Validation highlights:

  • year_levels must be non-empty positive ints
  • shared_with allowed only for lecture

docs.title: Staff
docs.text:
• _id, institution_id, department_id
  • name, email, role
  • faculty_id

docs.title: Availability
docs.text:
• _id, institution_id, staff_id, term_label
  • weekly_day_off
  • preferred_break_windows[]

docs.title: Room
docs.text:
• _id, institution_id, faculty_id
  • name, label, capacity
  • features[]

docs.title: MongoDB Collection Inputs
docs.text:
Primary collections read by solver route:

  • institutions
  • courses
  • users (filtered to role in [professor, ta])
  • availability
  • rooms
  • constraints

Soft-delete convention:

  • queries filter deleted_at: null

docs.title: Request
docs.text:
{
  "institution_id": "test-inst-001",
  "term_label": "fall-2024",
  "weights": {
    "break_window": 100,
    "consecutive_slots": 80,
    "session_spread": 60,
    "campus_clustering": 40
  }
}

docs.title: Response (trimmed)
docs.text:
{
  "snapshot_id": "uuid",
  "institution_id": "test-inst-001",
  "entries": [
    {
      "section_id": "sec-cs101-lec",
      "day_of_week": 1,
      "start_time": "09:00",
      "end_time": "10:00",
      "room_id": "room-101"
    }
  ],
  "hard_violations": 0,
  "soft_penalty": 320.0
}

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text:
This project has two deployment tracks:

  • backend API deployment to Azure Container Apps
  • documentation deployment to GitHub Pages via MkDocs workflow

docs.title: Backend: Azure Container Apps
docs.text:
Primary workflow file:

  • .github/workflows/deploy.yml

This workflow:

  1. runs tests
  2. builds and pushes Docker image to ACR
  3. updates the Azure Container App image
  4. probes /health endpoint after rollout

Required repository secrets:

  • AZURE_CREDENTIALS
  • REGISTRY_USERNAME
  • REGISTRY_PASSWORD

Required env values in workflow:

  • REGISTRY
  • IMAGE_NAME
  • RESOURCE_GROUP
  • CONTAINER_APP

docs.title: Docs: GitHub Pages + MkDocs
docs.text:
Primary workflow file:

  • .github/workflows/mkdocs-pages.yml

This workflow:

  1. installs MkDocs dependencies
  2. builds docs from mkdocs.yml
  3. uploads site/ artifact
  4. deploys artifact using actions/deploy-pages

docs.title: GitHub Pages Configuration
docs.text:
In repository settings, set:

  • Settings -> Pages -> Source -> GitHub Actions

If source is set to Deploy from a branch, GitHub can run branch-based Pages behavior that may publish root content (for example README.md) instead of MkDocs artifact output.

docs.title: Manual Deployment Commands (Local)
docs.text:
Build and run container locally:

docker build -t schedula-fastapi .
docker run --rm -p 8000:8000 \
    -e MONGODB_URI="mongodb+srv://..." \
    -e MONGODB_DB_NAME="schedula" \
    schedula-fastapi

docs.title: Operational Checks
docs.text:
After deployment, validate:

  • GET /health returns 200
  • GET /health/ready returns 200 and database=connected
  • POST /schedule/generate succeeds with real institution data

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
• Python 3.11+
  • MongoDB Atlas (or compatible MongoDB)
  • Optional: Docker and Docker Compose

docs.title: 1. Create Environment and Install Dependencies
docs.text:
python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
python -m pip install --upgrade pip
pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: 2. Configure Environment Variables
docs.text:
Create a .env file in the repository root.

Minimal required values:

MONGODB_URI=mongodb+srv://<user>:<password>@<cluster>/<db>?retryWrites=true&w=majority
MONGODB_DB_NAME=schedula
DEBUG=true

Optional tuning values are documented in Configuration.

docs.title: 3. Run the API
docs.text:
source .venv/bin/activate
uvicorn app.main:app --reload

Service URLs:

  • http://localhost:8000/
  • http://localhost:8000/health
  • http://localhost:8000/health/ready
  • http://localhost:8000/docs

docs.title: 4. Run MkDocs Locally
docs.text:
source .venv/bin/activate
mkdocs serve

Docs URL:

  • http://127.0.0.1:8000 (or next available port shown by MkDocs)

docs.title: 5. Run Tests
docs.text:
source .venv/bin/activate
pytest -q

docs.title: 6. Optional Docker Run
docs.text:
docker build -t schedula-fastapi .
docker run --rm -p 8000:8000 \
  -e MONGODB_URI="mongodb+srv://..." \
  -e MONGODB_DB_NAME="schedula" \
  schedula-fastapi

docs.title: Typical Developer Loop
docs.text:
1. Start API with uvicorn --reload
  2. Update code under app/
  3. Run pytest
  4. Update docs in docs/
  5. Preview docs with mkdocs serve

docs.title: Testing
docs.text: Test suite is based on pytest with async support.

docs.title: Test Configuration
docs.text:
pytest.ini:

  • asyncio_mode = auto

docs.title: Test Files
docs.text:
• tests/test_routes.py: route-level health/root checks
  • tests/test_solver.py: solver unit coverage
  • tests/test_integration.py: API integration against MongoDB and live server

docs.title: Run Commands
docs.text:
All tests:

pytest -q

Verbose:

pytest tests/ -v

Single file:

pytest tests/test_solver.py -v

Filter by keyword:

pytest -k "constraint" -v

docs.title: Route tests
docs.text:
• GET /health returns healthy payload
  • GET / returns basic service metadata

docs.title: Solver tests
docs.text:
• slot/day time-grid calculations
  • slots-per-week enforcement
  • room no-overlap
  • staff day-off rule
  • year-level conflict prevention
  • skipped sections when no compatible rooms
  • section duration precedence and overrides

docs.title: Integration tests
docs.text:
• seeded MongoDB collections are read correctly
  • /schedule/generate happy path and error path
  • weight override behavior in summary payload
  • hard-constraint invariants in generated entries

docs.title: Integration Test Prerequisites
docs.text:
Integration tests expect:

  • API reachable at http://localhost:8000
  • MongoDB configured and accessible via env settings

Typical flow:

uvicorn app.main:app --reload
pytest tests/test_integration.py -v

docs.title: CI
docs.text:
ci.yml executes:

  1. dependency install (pip install -r requirements.txt)
  2. pytest tests/ -v
  3. ruff check .

docs.title: CI/CD Workflows
docs.text:
Current GitHub Actions workflows in .github/workflows:

  • ci.yml
  • deploy.yml
  • mkdocs-pages.yml

docs.title: ci.yml (Continuous Integration)
docs.text:
Triggers:

  • push to main
  • pull requests targeting main

Jobs:

  • test: installs dependencies and runs pytest
  • lint: runs ruff check . after tests

Purpose:

  • validates backend correctness and code quality for every change

docs.title: deploy.yml (Azure Container Apps)
docs.text:
Name: Deploy to Azure Container Apps

Triggers:

  • push to main on selected backend paths
  • manual dispatch

Flow:

  1. run tests
  2. build/push container image to ACR
  3. update Azure Container App image
  4. verify service health endpoint

docs.title: mkdocs-pages.yml (Docs Deployment)
docs.text:
Name: Deploy MkDocs to GitHub Pages

Triggers:

  • push to main when docs or mkdocs.yml changes
  • manual dispatch

Flow:

  1. checkout and setup Pages context
  2. install mkdocs + theme
  3. build site from mkdocs.yml
  4. upload site artifact
  5. deploy artifact to GitHub Pages

docs.title: Pages Source Requirement
docs.text:
To ensure MkDocs artifact deployment is used:

  • set repository Settings -> Pages -> Source to GitHub Actions

If source is configured as Deploy from a branch, GitHub may run branch-based Pages deployment behavior (which can publish root/docs branch content rather than artifact-based MkDocs output).

docs.title: Cleanup Completed
docs.text: Obsolete docs workflow file was removed and replaced with mkdocs-pages.yml. The Azure deployment workflow was intentionally preserved.
